columns.fragility_note: Already normalised to [0,1] by upstream processing. Underlying source: INDPRO (Fed industrial production index).
columns.manipulability_note: INDPRO base year revisions; seasonal adjustment methodology.

columns.fragility_note: Already normalised. Underlying source: TCU (total capacity utilisation, Fed).
columns.manipulability_note: TCU survey coverage changes across industries over time.

columns.fragility_note: Already normalised. Underlying source: T10YFF (10Y-FF spread, macro integration proxy).
columns.manipulability_note: Financial spread is market-determined; reflects risk sentiment, not only structural integration.

columns.fragility_note: Already normalised. Underlying source: CPIAUCSL (CPI all urban consumers, monthly).
columns.manipulability_note: CPI methodology changes (housing imputation, substitution bias) create level shifts.

columns.fragility_note: Already normalised. Underlying source: M2SL (M2 money supply, Fed) — monetary symbolic stock proxy.
columns.manipulability_note: M2 definition changed in May 2020 (savings accounts reclassification). Structural break.

notes: US macro monthly panel (FRED). All five ORI-C columns (O, R, I, demand, S) are pre-normalised to [0,1] by the upstream resolve_real_datasets.py pipeline. No re-normalisation needed. Raw sources: INDPRO (O), TCU (R), T10YFF (I), CPIAUCSL (demand), M2SL (S). Monthly frequency 1986-present. M2 structural break May 2020 flagged in event_calendar.
